thesis: Leading all-flash enterprise data storage provider formerly known as Pure Storage (PSTG). Rebranded to Everpure in February 2026 with single-letter ticker 'P' change in April 2026. FY2026 revenue of $3.66B (+15.6% YoY) with gross margin expansion to 70.4% and first year of GAAP profitability ($188.2M net income). Strong FCF generation of $690.6M and $2.39B cash balance. Evergreen//One subscription model and AI infrastructure positioning provide durable growth. However, stock has rallied sharply post-rebrand with RSI near overbought and forward P/E of ~29x leaving limited margin of safety. Significant SBC dilution ($481.7M annually) remains a headwind.

drivers: Revenue grows at 20% CAGR through FY2030 driven by AI infrastructure demand
Operating margins expand to 22%+ as subscription mix increases and scale benefits accrue
1touch acquisition integration accelerates data intelligence revenue stream
Evergreen//One subscription reaches 40%+ of revenue, driving multiple expansion

risks: Revenue growth slows to 10% CAGR due to cloud migration and competitive pressure
Operating margins stall at 14% due to pricing pressure and SBC inflation
Enterprise IT spending downturn compresses storage demand cyclically
Valuation compresses to 20x forward earnings on execution concerns

base_case.assumptions: Revenue grows at 15% CAGR through FY2030; Gross margins stabilize at 69-71%; Operating margins reach 18% by FY2030; Subscription mix grows to 35% of revenue; FCF generation sustains $700M+ annually.

catalysts.event: Q1 FY2027 Earnings — First post-rebrand guidance validation
1touch Acquisition Close — Expands data intelligence TAM
Everpure Investor Day — Long-term strategic roadmap
Q3 FY2027 Earnings — Full-year guidance update

key_risks: Stock has rallied ~20% in two weeks post-rebrand; RSI near overbought at 70.9
$481.7M annual stock-based compensation creates ongoing shareholder dilution
$2.78B convertible notes create potential future dilution if converted
Intense competition from Dell EMC, NetApp, and hyperscaler cloud storage
Enterprise storage is cyclical; recession would materially impact demand
